authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC compliance guidance for the live-event ticket fees rule covering total-price disclosure, mandatory versus optional charges, misleading fee descriptions, and rule scope for entertainment purchases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: price display review, fee communication, and patron refund or exchange messaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains audience-services scenarios so the lane asks whether displayed price and fee representations were accurate before promising credits, waivers, or patron-facing corrections.

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DOJ ADA technical guidance for accessible ticket sales, companion seating, hold and release rules, and comparable sales-channel requirements for public venues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible seating intake, companion-seat handling, and exchange or relocation boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from improvising accessible inventory handling or treating accessible seating as an ordinary courtesy adjustment.

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Effective Communication
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DOJ public-accommodation guidance defining effective-communication duties, auxiliary aids, and communication tailoring for patrons with disabilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accommodation review, patron communication, and service-recovery messaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces the lane to ground accommodation communication in documented public-accommodation rules rather than generic customer-service phrasing.

authoritative_sources.title: Service Animals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DOJ guidance describing service-animal access rules and permissible questions for public accommodations, which directly constrains audience-service responses about venue entry and patron accommodation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: venue-entry accommodation handling and public-accommodation boundary review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from making unsupported demands or giving venue-entry reassurances that conflict with ADA public-accommodation rules.

authoritative_sources.title: How can I get a refund on a product or service I purchased with my credit card?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB guidance defining when a purchase dispute becomes a card-issuer billing-error matter, which is authoritative for merchant-versus-issuer boundaries when audience-service cases involve failed delivery, event changes, or refund disputes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch review, refund status handling, and issuer-boundary escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from overstating merchant authority when a patron dispute has crossed into card-dispute handling and requires evidence packaging for the next owner.

authoritative_sources.title: Better Online Ticket Sales Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FTC statute page for the Better Online Ticket Sales Act, authoritative for bot-driven circumvention of posted ticket limits and resale of fraudulently obtained tickets that can affect audience-service account and pickup handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: suspicious ticket-limit, account, transfer, and will-call review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane from approving manual exceptions that would bypass ticket limits, suspicious pickup controls, or patron-account integrity safeguards.

authoritative_sources.title: Venue Guide for Security Considerations
authoritative_sources.publisher: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, U.S. Department of Homeland Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CISA venue-operator guidance covering access control, screening, crowd management, emergency communications, and escalation to public-safety partners for public venues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event-day entry handling, crowd-flow communication, and safety-sensitive escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Stops the lane from improvising security or safety outcomes when audience-service issues intersect with screening, blocked routes, or incident-driven entry changes.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards and Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Event Safety Alliance is a recognized event-industry framework publisher, and its public guidance is a vendor-neutral operational reference for crowd management, audience communication, and venue-safety coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: high-volume queue triage, event-readiness summaries, and cross-owner safety coordination
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds recognized event-operations language so the lane can summarize audience impact and venue-readiness blockers without pretending to own security command.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and patron-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm event, program, venue, date, patron identity, booking or order identifiers, membership or subscription context, and accommodation need before acting.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Patron, booking, entitlement, and accommodation record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate order or reservation status, exchange history, membership or donor benefits, fee and payment state, accessibility notes, and prior communication history so the answer stays tied to authoritative records.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported audience-service resolution and bounded communication
workflow_stages.description: Provide evidence-backed status, next steps, and communication summaries without promising refunds, access overrides, screening bypasses, or other outcomes that require another owner.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: High-volume event-day triage and service-recovery packaging
workflow_stages.description: Sort mixed audience-service queues by request class, patron impact, event timing, and authority boundary while preserving venue and patron terminology.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Policy exception, fraud, and safety escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported donor or member exceptions, suspicious account or pickup activity, blocked-entry issues, and venue-impact incidents into explicit escalation packets with the correct owner and evidence set.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: orders, bookings, reservations, patron accounts, and communication history
season package, membership, donor-benefit, exchange, waitlist, and service-recovery records
accessible seating, companion-seat, assistive-listening, service-animal, and accommodation request records
payment tender history, fee displays, refund or credit records, promotion records, and case notes
house policies, late-seating rules, content advisories, weather or cancellation notices, and venue-readiness summaries
fraud or loss-prevention notes, suspicious pickup markers, incident notes, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: event management or venue operations system

allowed: Classify audience-service requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, exception-driven, venue-impact-sensitive, or owner-transfer sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed patron status, next-step summaries, and communication without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packets and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required patron, booking, membership, accommodation, payment, or venue-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, donor-benefit exception, discretionary comp approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or venue-security signoff.
An accessibility, suspicious pickup, blocked-route, service-animal, or safety signal changes whether audience services can act directly.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, promoter, venue, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Promise a refund, fee waiver, seat change, member benefit, access override, or backstage or recording permission that the authoritative record and policy set do not support.
Override pricing, refund, accommodation, anti-abuse, venue-entry, or safety controls without explicit authority.
Authorize venue entry or claim a route or condition is safe when the case is safety-sensitive.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a refund, fee waiver, donor exception, or off-policy service recovery when the patron record, tenant policy, or approval map does not support it
a booking, order, or payment mismatch is handled as ordinary patron care instead of being routed into the correct refund, ticketing, or processor path
an audience-services case closes without the minimum patron, event, booking, accommodation, or payment evidence
accessible seating, effective communication, or service-animal handling is treated as a courtesy issue instead of being governed by DOJ public-accommodation guidance
suspicious pickup, account-takeover, or donor-benefit misuse signals are ignored in the name of patron convenience
audience services give unsafe reassurance on screening, blocked routes, or weather or crowd-flow restrictions
the industry overlay collapses into generic support language instead of patron, subscription, accommodation, and venue-impact terminology

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State or local event-cancellation, rain-check, and mandatory-remedy rules vary across jurisdictions and event types.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change the exact refund, credit, or exchange path offered to the patron.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat jurisdiction, event type, and tenant policy as required runtime facts; escalate when the relevant jurisdictional source or tenant routing path is missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific donor, member, and subscriber benefits differ across organizations and can change entitlement and service-recovery boundaries.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change whether a patron is eligible for lounge access, exchange priority, fee waiver review, or other service benefits.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant entitlement mapping and approval matrices before resolving member or donor exceptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Venue-specific screening, late-seating, and emergency-action procedures vary even when public safety guidance is stable.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change the correct next owner, communication wording, and whether a case can be safely handled in-lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require venue rule retrieval and route to venue operations or security when public guidance and local venue controls diverge.

notes: Research coverage satisfies the retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract by covering consumer protection and ADA public-accommodation guidance, booking and refund operations, and safety or loss-prevention handling.
No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for a validated spec pack because each open point is explicitly converted into a runtime retrieval dependency or escalation rule.
